properties.remark: REPORTS WERE RECEIVED OF WATER ENTERING BUSINESSES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CONGRESS AND GATEWAY. A STORM SPOTTER IN BOYNTON BEACH REPORTED IN EXCESS OF FIVE INCHES OF RAIN IN ABOUT 2 HOURS BEFORE THE RAIN GAUGE OVERFLOWED. MEDIA REPORTS OF DOZENS OF STALLED CARS AND 40 VEHICLE ACCIDENTS DUE TO THE RAIN AND STANDING WATER. BOYNTON BEACH BOULEVARD WAS CLOSED JUST WEST OF I-95, AS WERE THE I-95 ONRAMPS AT BOYNTON BEACH AND GATEWAY BOULEVARDS.
PUBLIC REPORTED A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN WEST OF I-95 AND BOYNTON BEACH BLVD. REPORT RELAYED TO BOYNTON BEACH POLICE DEPARTMENT. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.

NWS SURVEY ESTIMATES TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOMEWHERE AROUND OR JUST NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF I-595 AND SW 31ST AVENUE AND MOVED NORTH NORTHEAST ALONG SW 31ST AVENUE BEFORE LIFINTG OFF THE GROUND SOMEWHERE JUST SOUTH OF SUNRISE BLVD. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROX. 2.5 TO 3 MILES. ITS WIDEST PATH WAS ABOUT 1/8 OF A MILE BETWEEN SW 4 ST AND SW 8 ST...OR BETWEEN DAVIE AND BROWARD BLVD. MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES...ROOFS...AND OUTSIDE OBJECTS NOTED.

EF1 RATED TORNADO WITH HIGHEST ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS NEAR 90 MPH. TORNADO TRACK BEGAN AT W 22ND STREET JUST WEST OF W 1 AVE WHERE VEHICLES WERE MOVED AND FLIPPED AT A TOW YARD. ROOF DAMAGE TO AT LEAST FIVE SEPARATE APARTMENT COMPLEXES, WITH WORST DAMAGE BETWEEN 22ND AND 24TH STREETS. TORNADO LIFTED AT W 28 STREET BETWEEN PALM AVENUE AND W 1 AVE. VERY NARROW PATH OF NO MORE THAN 15 YARDS. PATH LENGTH 0.4 MILES.

SMALL TORNADO BRIEFLY TRACKED ACROSS HALLANDALE BEACH SOUTH OF HALLANDALE BEACH BOULEVARD AND EAST OF I-95. DAMAGE TO PATIO SCREEN ENCLOSURE, ALUMINUM BOATS FLIPPED, TREES AND TREE BRANCHES DOWNED. LOW-END EF0 WITH HIGHEST WINDS 65 MPH. TRACK LENGTH 0.2 MILES.

UPDATE 8/20 AFTER STORM SURVEY - LOCAL EMPLOYEES ESTIMATED DAMAGE AT EQUINE CLINIC AROUND 130 AM EDT FROM TORNADO.TORNADO STARTED NEAR POLO PARK MIDDLE SCHOOL AND TRAVELED NORTHWEST FOR ABOUT 2.75 MILES,LIFTING NEAR WELLINGTON HIGH SCHOOL. DAMAGE PATH ABOUT 70 TO 80 YARDS WIDE AT WIDEST POINT.MANY TREES DOWN,TILE ROOFS STRIPPED CLEAN, EQUINE CLINIC STABLES ROOFS COMPLETELY REMOVED, POWER POLE SNAPPED HALFWAY UP ON PIERSON ROAD.

SPOTTER REPORTED TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CARLTON ST AND GREEN VIEW SHORES BLVD. SPOTTER REPORTED SLIDING GLASS DOOR IN THE FRONT OF THE HOUSE BLEW IN ACCOMPANYING ROARING SOUND. NEIGHBOR HAD DAMAGE ALSO AND POWER IS OUT IN THE NEIGHBORDHOOD. EVENT HAPPENED JUST SOUTH OF WELLINGTON HIGH SCHOOL.
